stimulus: Renowned Japanese ceramicist Shōji Hamada (1894–1978) created his signature high-gloss stoneware using a glazing technique called salt firing. This technique involves throwing rock salt into the kiln at peak temperatures. ______blank the vapor settles on the clay surface, creating a glossy, mottled glaze.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "As a result" logically signals that the information in this sentence—the salt vapor creating a glossy glaze—is a result of the salt-firing technique described in the previous sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because "on the contrary" illogically signals opposition. Choice C is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals exemplification. Choice D is incorrect because "previously" illogically signals that the glazing occurs before the salt is thrown, when in fact it follows.
